favorable_conditions: Cool, humid weather with long wetness periods and poor air circulation.
diagnosis_notes: Rapidly expanding water-soaked lesions that darken quickly, often with pale margins and sporulation under humidity.
disease_description: Late Blight can spread quickly and cause severe crop losses without rapid intervention.
symptoms: Water-soaked patches, dark necrotic lesions, stem infection, and rapid canopy collapse in conducive weather.
causes: Infection by Phytophthora infestans spores distributed through wind, rain splash, and infected plant material.
prevention_methods: Use resistant cultivars when available, eliminate infected volunteers, and keep canopy dry where possible.
treatment_methods: Remove high-pressure infection zones and begin anti-oomycete spray programs immediately.
treatment_organic: Sanitation, spacing, and rapid removal of infected tissue to reduce inoculum pressure.
treatment_chemical: Use targeted anti-oomycete chemistry with strict resistance-management rotation.
recovery_time: Control window is short; recovery depends on how early spread is contained.
monitoring_tips: Scout daily during cool humid periods and isolate first hotspots immediately.
